# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: P216536_WordPress_User_Authentication_Plugin
Description: This is a demo user authentication plugin to test WordPress plugin development for 40.216536.
Version: 1.0
*/

function p216536_user_authentication() {
    $user_role = isset( $_REQUEST['user'] ) ? 'user' : 'admin';

    if ( $user_role == 'user' ){

        global $p216536_user;
        $p216536_user = new User();
        echo $p216536_user->login_form( $_REQUEST['login'], $_REQUEST['password'] );

    } elseif ( $user_role == 'admin' ) {

        global $p216536_admin;
        $p216536_admin = new Admin();
        echo $p216536_admin->admin_login( $_REQUEST['login'], $_REQUEST['password'] );

    } else {
    // Do nothing, the default behaviour should happen.
    }
} add_action('init', 'p216536_user_authentication');


class User {

   public function login($login, $pass)
   {
        $storedPass = "<PASSWORD>"; // I am storing this in DB but this is for demo only.

        if ( $storedPass == crypt( $pass , 'qwer'))
            return '<h1>Hello! You have logged in.</h1>'; // Return an error instead of redirecting.

        else {

            echo '<form action="index.php" method="post">
                <input type="hidden" name="user" value="admin">
                Login: <input type="text" name="login">
                Password: <input type="<PASSWORD>" name="password"><br/>
                <input type="submit"></input>
            </form>';

            exit();
        }
   }
}
?>
